Committee.CommitteeName: Special Interim Committee on Property Tax Reform
Special Committee on Property Tax Reform

RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 115-H, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Matthew Overcast, a Republican, represents Douglas, Ozark, southern Stone, and Taney Counties (District 155) in the Missouri House of Representatives. He was elected to his first term in November of 2024.
Overcast, born in Houston, Missouri, completed his primary and secondary education in Ozark and Douglas County, eventually graduating from Ava High School. He earned an Associate degree in Legal Studies and a Bachelor of Science degree in Criminal Justice from Drury University, followed by a Doctor of Jurisprudence from the Mitchell Hamline School of Law.
Overcast served in the United States Air Force from 2010 to 2014 as a Tactical Aircraft Journeyman and Dedicated Crew Chief, where he received an Air Force Achievement Medal. Overcast is a business professional with experience in executive leadership and as a Missouri small business owner. Prior to his legal career, he worked as a probation officer and law clerk. In 2024, Overcast started his own private law practice, with a focus on business and education law, where he continues to practice today.
Overcast is involved with several organizations, including the Council of Parent Attorneys and Advocates (COPAA), Missouri Bar Association, the Minnesota Bar Association, the American Legion, the NRA, and the Ava Chamber of Commerce.
Representative Overcast resides on his family farm in Ava, Missouri with his wife Jessica, a local schoolteacher, and their five children: Lyla, Josephine, Adalynn, William, and Jackson.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies provisions related to county sales tax increases
Authorizes the county assessor to provide taxpayers with a form to verify structures on parcels of real property within a volunteer fire protection association's service area
Modifies the statute of limitations for personal injury claims from five years to two years
Modifies provisions relating to the collateral source rule
Modifies definition of commercial feed to include industrial hemp
Establishes and modifies provisions relating to court reporters
Modifies provisions relating to advanced practice registered nurses
Modifies provisions relating to alternative therapies and treatments, including psilocybin
Establishes provisions relating to payments for tort liability to insurers
Modifies provisions relating to actions for damages due to exposure to asbestos
Modifies provisions relating to the list of diseases required to be reported to the department of health and senior services
Modifies provisions relating to assistant physicians
Creates new provisions relating to joint employers
Authorizes counties of the third classification to use up to fifty percent of collected court costs and court fees for courthouse construction or renovation projects over a 7 year period
Repeals provisions relating to certificates of need
Modifies provisions relating to the Alzheimer's state plan task force

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies provisions relating to home school protections
Lowers the age for adult high schools from 21 to 18
Establishes the "Missouri Religious Freedom Protection Act"
Establishes the "Defining SEX Act"
Modifies provisions relating to income tax
Designates Branson as the "Live Entertainment Capital of Missouri"
Modifies provisions relating to the rights of unborn children
Prohibits the use of fetal organs or tissue resulting from elective abortions for medical, scientific, research, experimental, or therapeutic purposes or any other use
Establishes provisions for the appointment and duties of commissioners to attend an Article V convention
Provides for MO HealthNet coverage of hearing aids and cochlear implants
Prohibits abortions following the detection of a fetal heartbeat and modifies provisions relating to the rights of unborn children
Creates the "Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act"
Creates provisions relating to alternative therapies for veterans with PTSD and traumatic brain injuries
Requires public school districts and charter schools to prevent student use of electronic personal communications devices during regularly scheduled instructional activities
Creates provisions relating to the regulation of hemp-derived consumable products
Prohibits student use of telecommunications devices during regularly scheduled instructional activities
Establishes the "Anti-Red Flag Gun Seizure Act"
Establishes written parental consent requirements for individualized education programs (IEPs)
Establishes the "Entrepreneur Rights Act", exempting certain businesses from minimum wage increases and paid sick leave provisions
Requires the general assembly to approve proposed administrative rules
Modifies provisions relating to nondisclosure agreements in childhood sexual abuse cases
Modifies provisions relating to telemedicine
Modifies provisions relating to firearms
Increases the age from seventeen to eighteen as it relates to proceedings involving orders of protection
Modifies provisions relating to the protection of children
Establishes provisions governing duties and responsibilities of public entities
Modifies provisions relating to licensure of child care facilities
Provides protections against discrimination and antisemitism in public schools and public postsecondary educational institutions
Modifies provisions relating to the protection of children
Establishes the "Zero-Cost Adoption Fund Act" which establishes a fund to offset costs associated with adoption and a tax credit for any person who contributes to the fund
Prohibits a political subdivision from adopting policies for property transferred by political subdivisions from being used for lawful education purposes by a charter school
Authorizes the state Board of Education to issue certificates of license to teach upon certification by Teachers for Tomorrow
Reestablishes the "Second Amendment Preservation Act"
Prohibits the use of fetal organs or tissue resulting from abortions for medical, scientific, research, experimental, or therapeutic purposes or any other use
Prohibits a political subdivision from adopting policies for property transferred by political subdivisions from being used for lawful education purposes by a charter school
Prohibits a political subdivision from adopting policies for property transferred by political subdivisions from being used for lawful education purposes by a charter school

Requires physicians prescribing abortion medication to provide information to the woman seeking the abortion about reversal medication and requires the posting of such information in several public areas
Designates the last week in April each year as "Infertility Awareness Week"

Proposes a constitutional amendment to impose an appropriation spending limitation and to establish the "Tax Reform Fund" to be used to fund budgetary shortfalls, subject to an appropriation limitation, implement sales tax changes, and allow for certain taxation changes based on revenue triggers, by general law
Proposes a constitutional amendment that establishes provisions relating to reproductive health care
